message:
[MEDIUM] fcp-protocol: tighten retention_for_schema namespace matching (dot-delimited)

retention_for_schema used plain `ns.starts_with(prefix)` against the
REQUIRED_PREFIXES and EPHEMERAL_PREFIXES tables. A namespace like
`fcp.heartbeat-evil` or `fcp.healthy` passed the ephemeral check and was
classified as droppable — evading the "Required for unknown" default
audit retention.

Switch to dot-delimited matching: exact match OR `{prefix}.{suffix}`.
Keeps legitimate sub-namespaces like `fcp.invoke.v2` intact, blocks
arbitrary byte-prefix impersonation.

Required-direction prefixes gain the same discipline. The fail-safe
direction already produced the right answer (unknown => Required), so
behavior is unchanged for any namespace not specifically crafted to
evade audit — but the test locks it in so future refactors can't
reintroduce prefix ambiguity.

Tests:
- retention_subnamespace_with_dot_inherits_retention: `fcp.invoke.v2`
  and `fcp.heartbeat.sub` resolve to Required / Ephemeral respectively.
- retention_near_prefix_does_not_inherit_retention: `fcp.heartbeat-evil`,
  `fcp.healthy`, and `fcp.invoker` all fall to the Required default.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-protocol --lib retention_ → 30 passed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
